failure_surface: All consecutive rows with matching sheet name (E='SH1') received the same invoice number instead of being divided into separate logical blocks requiring distinct invoice numbers.
root_cause: The agent's logic tracked block transitions only when switching between different sheet names in column E, but did not recognize intra-block boundaries within the same sheet name (e.g., TOTAL rows or natural breaks). This caused all SH1-matching rows to be treated as one continuous group, receiving only the first extracted invoice number repeatedly.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Implement detection of intra-sheet-name block boundaries using semantic markers such as 'TOTAL' in adjacent columns or row-by-row data patterns, so each logical group of rows receives its own sequential invoice number from the SH1 source.
evidence: Agent extracted 4 invoice numbers ['A7179', 'A7180', 'A7185', 'A7188'] from SH1 but only used A7179 for all modified rows
Execution logs show: 'Row 2: Set D=A7179' through 'Row 13: Set D=A7179' - all consecutive SH1 rows received identical value
Agent's state logic only incremented inv_idx when exiting SH1 block: 'elif not current_is_sh1 and in_sh1_block: inv_idx += 1'
DATA sheet inspection revealed multiple PTMR entries (PTMR100, PTMR101, PTMR102, PTMR103) which should correspond to distinct invoice number blocks

memory_items.title: Block Boundary Detection Within Same Sheet Name
memory_items.lesson: When mapping external reference values to rows grouped by a common key, do not assume all consecutive matching rows belong to one block. Detect internal boundaries using semantic markers like totals, empty cells, or changes in adjacent columns.
memory_items.generalization: Always scan for intra-group delimiters (e.g., 'TOTAL' rows, value resets, pattern changes) when processing logically-segmented data within a worksheet.

memory_items.title: Verify Source Count Matches Target Distribution Needs
memory_items.lesson: Before applying source values (e.g., invoice numbers, identifiers) to target ranges, calculate how many distinct groups exist in the target and confirm sufficient source values are available.
memory_items.generalization: Validate alignment between the count of extractable source items and the expected number of target groups before writing operations begin.

memory_items.title: Semantic Markers Over Simple Equality Checks
memory_items.lesson: Grouping by equality of one column alone may incorrectly merge distinct entities. Use multi-column analysis to identify true segment boundaries.
memory_items.generalization: When iterating over target rows, track both the primary grouping key AND secondary indicators (totals, IDs, status markers) to determine when a new logical block begins.

edits.content:
### Block Boundary Detection
- When distributing values from one sheet/range to another grouped by a common identifier, do not treat all consecutive matching rows as a single block.
- Look for semantic delimiters within the data: 'TOTAL' rows, empty separator rows, or changes in related columns that indicate a new logical group.
- Calculate the expected number of groups before assigning source values; validate that enough distinct source items exist.
- State management: Track transitions within the same key value as well as between different key values to correctly sequence distributed values.
